title: What is a Language Model?
concept: A language model learns the probability distribution of sequences, letting it predict the next word given a context history.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a language model?
exercises.explain: Language models predict sequences of words, making them the core technology behind writing assistants and chatbots.

cards.title: Mind-Reading Tech?
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how smart speakers, chatbots, and email auto-completers seem to know exactly what you are about to say? They aren't magical minds—they are using math to predict the next word!

cards.title: What is a Language Model?
cards.body: A Language Model is a smart system that learns the patterns of human language. By looking at thousands of books, articles, and websites, it figures out the mathematical probability of words coming after one another. Its main job is to guess the next word in a sequence!

cards.title: The Predictive Keyboard
cards.body: Imagine you are texting a friend and type "Happy..." Your phone's keyboard immediately suggests "Birthday" or "New Year". It doesn't know your life story; it just knows which words usually follow "Happy" based on millions of texts it has seen before. A language model is like a super-powered version of this predictive keyboard.

cards.title: Predicting the Next Word
cards.body:
If you give a language model the starting phrase: "The cat sat on the...", it calculates the chance of different words coming next:

• "mat" (Very High Probability)
• "floor" (Medium Probability)
• "banana" (Very Low Probability)

It chooses one of the highly probable words to continue the sentence naturally.

cards.title: Myth: They Are Alive!
cards.body: Many people believe language models are conscious and actually "understand" the meaning of life. In reality, they don't think or feel. They are simply calculators that are extremely good at guessing which words look correct together based on their training data.

cards.title: Meaning vs. Math
cards.body: Unlike humans who understand ideas, a language model works entirely on calculating probabilities and patterns of words.

left.text: Understands concepts, has feelings, and knows the real-world meaning behind words.

right.text: Calculates probabilities to guess the next word based on patterns it has seen.

exercises.prompt: How does a language model generate a sentence?

options.text: It memorizes every sentence ever written.
It calculates the probability of the next word based on previous words.
It randomly selects words from a dictionary.
It translates words into secret code numbers that humans can't read.

exercises.explain: Language models look at the context of previous words to calculate which word is most likely to follow next.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Language models can feel emotions and truly understand the sentences they write.
exercises.explain: Language models do not understand feelings or concepts; they just calculate which words are most likely to follow other words.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of a language model's main job.
exercises.sentence: A language model's main job is to [blank] the next word in a sequence.
exercises.explain: Language models are designed to predict the next word in a sequence based on the context of the words that came before it.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using a language model?

options.text: A spreadsheet calculator adding up a column of numbers.
A robot assistant suggesting a polite reply to an email.
A computer folder organizing files by date created.

exercises.explain: The robot assistant is predicting the most likely words to form a polite response, whereas the calculator and the file organizer are not predicting language sequences.

exercises.prompt: How does a language model actually decide which word to output next?

options.text: It searches a giant database for pre-written sentences.
It predicts the next word based on mathematical probabilities.
It reads your mind using hardware sensors.
It randomly picks any word from the dictionary.

exercises.explain: A language model calculates probabilities to determine which word is most likely to follow a given set of words, rather than looking up exact pre-written answers from a database.
